notes: Bundling small purchases into monthly credit card charges; model used by Qpass and others
Billing digital purchases through ISP or telephone bill; Aberdeen predicted this would be strong; succeeded in mobile (carrier billing)
Failed in U.S. (no viable business case for banks per MasterCard); succeeded internationally (France/Germany/UK/South Africa)
Embedded in PDAs, cell phones, set-top boxes, laptops; Wave Systems model
Heavy-wallet approaches failing; lighter versions emerging; CDNow cited; direct ancestor of Apple Pay/Google Pay
CyberGold model of earn-and-spend digital credits; evolved into modern loyalty/rewards programs
DigiCash/eCash model requiring bank buy-in; never achieved critical mass
Non-physical credit account aggregating payments; evolved into modern prepaid cards
Content protection and pay-per-use models; InterTrust was pioneer; DRM now standard in streaming
Compaq's micropayment broker scrip system; discontinued circa 2001
MasterCard subsidiary smart card; U.S. adoption failed per report; trials in UK/Canada; discontinued
